# EX09 Data Analysis Project

## My idea

For this project, I analyzed whether COMP110 should add optional short pre-lecture videos before harder topics. My idea is not that lecture is useless. I think lecture is still important, but programming is not always easy to learn only by watching.

Many exercises already give students chance to practice, but if students know the basic idea before class, lecture time can be more useful for practice, questions, and checking understanding.

## Why I chose this idea

I chose this idea because the survey has direct data about pre-lecture videos. I can also compare it with student understanding, difficulty, and how useful students think programming practice is.

## Chart 1: Interest in pre-lecture videos

![Student Interest in Pre-Lecture Videos](static/chart1.png)

This chart shows how students rated the idea of optional pre-lecture videos. If many students gave high ratings, then it suggests that students may want another way to prepare before lecture.

## Chart 2: Making own examples and understanding

![Making Own Code Examples and Understanding](static/chart2.png)

This chart compares how often students try to make their own code examples with their understanding. I used this because programming is more active than just watching code.

## Chart 3: Programming assignments and course value

![Programming Assignments and Course Value](static/chart3.png)

This chart compares how effective students think programming assignments are with how valuable they think the course is. This helps show whether hands-on practice connects with students feeling the course is useful.

## Conclusion

Overall, the data gives some support for my idea, but it does not fully prove it. If many students rate pre-lecture videos highly, then it shows students may want another way to prepare before lecture. The other charts also connect with my point that programming is easier to learn when students actually try code by themselves.

I think optional short videos can be useful because they do not force everyone to study the same way. Students who already feel ready can ignore them, but students who need more time can use them before lecture.

There are also trade-offs. Making videos takes extra time from instructors, and too many videos may feel like extra homework. So I think the videos should be short, optional, and only for harder topics. In the future, the course can collect better data by asking students if they actually watched the videos and whether the videos helped them feel more prepared.
